checks.a_analysis: I have compared the output text with the source PDF images and OCR. Every paragraph, heading, and table entry in the output has a direct corresponding element in the source document. There are no invented section headings or body text.
There is no contact information (phone numbers, emails, addresses, or URLs) present in either the source PDF or the output JSON, so no fabrication has occurred.

The source document consists of two pages. Page 1 contains the commission name, program title, minimum SPA amounts table, policy text, and the submittal schedule table. Page 2 contains instructions and screenshots. All of these components are present in the output JSON structure.

The first table 'MINIMUM SPA AMOUNTS' contains a header row and 4 data rows in the source; the output contains exactly the same number of rows and columns. The second table 'MONTHLY SPA SUBMITTAL SCHEDULE' contains a header row and 12 data rows (one for each month) in the source; the output contains exactly the same number of rows and columns.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: All paragraphs from the source are present in the output. Key text such as the notification requirements (90/60 days), inventory requirements, and the specific wording of the disclaimer footer are fully preserved. Bold text formatting for key terms like 'six' and 'minimum of one month' is also correctly captured.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: The text content matches the source document faithfully. No wording changes that alter the meaning of the instructions or policy were found. The output accurately reflects the source's inclusion of asterisks for policy notes and specific numerical values for discounts and dates.

checks.a_analysis: I have reviewed the entire document content. Each text block, paragraph, and table from the source PDF appears exactly once in the output JSON in its corresponding section. The metadata '01/2025' which appears multiple times in the source header/footer is correctly excluded from the main content elements, and no other content is repeated.

The document title is used as the root section title. Major section headings 'MINIMUM SPA AMOUNTS:' and 'MONTHLY SPA SUBMITTAL SCHEDULE:' are assigned H3. While this skips H2, the acceptable changes list allows for heading level adjustments for normalization and accessibility. The relative hierarchy is consistent as both sibling sections use the same level. The second page is correctly nested as a subsection 'SPA INSTRUCTIONS'.

There are two tables in the document. Table 1 (Minimum SPA Amounts) correctly identifies 2 columns and 5 rows (1 header, 4 data) as per the source. Table 2 (Monthly SPA Submittal Schedule) correctly identifies 2 columns and 13 rows (1 header, 12 data) as per the source. Row and column structure matches perfectly.

The reading order in the output JSON follows the top-to-bottom, page-by-page flow of the source PDF. It moves from the title to the first table, through the descriptive paragraphs, to the second table, then to the instructions on page 2. This is logically sound and faithful to the original intent.
The source document contains no explicitly bulleted or numbered lists. The instructions on page 2 are presented as distinct paragraphs, and the output JSON preserves this structural choice.
The source document contains no hyperlinks or URLs. The output JSON correctly reflects this by not injecting any manufactured links.

checks.a_analysis: The HTML output maintains the visual organization of the source PDF. The sequence of headings, tables, and paragraphs follows the original document's structure perfectly. While fonts and spacing differ as expected for HTML, the flow of information remains consistent and logical.

The tables in the document (Minimum SPA Amounts and Monthly SPA Submittal Schedule) are correctly rendered as HTML tables. Columns are properly aligned, and headers are visually distinct. The data remains readable and understandable. The UI screenshots containing tabular data are correctly preserved as images.
All meaningful images from the source PDF, specifically the three screenshots of the pricing system UI on page 2, are present and correctly rendered in the HTML output.

There is no visual duplication of body content paragraphs or tables. While some heading text is repeated to serve as table headers/captions (e.g., 'MINIMUM SPA AMOUNTS'), this is a standard practice for accessible document conversion and does not constitute a defect.
